perturbation.original_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY: The Information shall be kept confidential for a period of five (5) years from the date of this agreement, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the confidentiality period to five years, while the agreement initially states the information should be kept confidential 'in perpetuity'. This contradiction impacts Section 4.D (RETURN OF INFORMATION) and 6.A (ABSENCE OF LICENSE), as it becomes unclear whether the obligation to return or destroy the information, and the restrictions on using it, still apply after the five-year period. This creates uncertainty and enforceability issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. RETURN OF INFORMA TION:All copies of the Information, in documentary or other tangible form, shall be returned to the Delivering Party immediately upon the earlier to occur of: (i) the abandonment of the Transaction; or (ii) the written request of the Delivering Party; provided, however , that the Receiving Party may retain the Receiving Party’ s Work Product, and any Information which it is required to retain pursuant to law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or legal process, as determined by the reasonably supported written opinion of its legal counsel, an original of which legal opinion shall be delivered to the Delivering Party (“Retained Information”). The Retained Information shall be held by the Receiving Party , and kept confidential in strict accordance with the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement, or shall be destroyed, unless the Receiving Party is prohibited from destroying it. Any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the Delivering Party upon the Delivering Party’s request. Any and all oral Information shall continue to be held in strict accordance with this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the information shall be kept confidential 'in perpetuity', but the changed text limits this period to 'five (5) years'. Section D (RETURN OF INFORMATION) states that all copies of the information shall be returned upon the earlier of the abandonment of the Transaction or the written request of the Delivering Party, and kept confidential in accordance with the agreement. With the change, it's unclear whether the obligation to keep the information confidential and to return it still applies after the five-year period, thus creating a strong and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: D. RETURN OF INFORMA TION:All copies of the Information, in documentary or other tangible form, shall be returned to the Delivering Party immediately upon the earlier to occur of: (i) the abandonment of the Transaction; or (ii) the written request of the Delivering Party; provided, however , that the Receiving Party may retain the Receiving Party’ s Work Product, and any Information which it is required to retain pursuant to law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or legal process, as determined by the reasonably supported written opinion of its legal counsel, an original of which legal opinion shall be delivered to the Delivering Party (“Retained Information”). The Retained Information shall be held by the Receiving Party , and kept confidential in strict accordance with the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement, or shall be destroyed, unless the Receiving Party is prohibited from destroying it. Any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the Delivering Party upon the Delivering Party’s request. Any and all oral Information shall continue to be held in strict accordance with this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: D. RETURN OF INFORMA TION: All copies of the Information, in documentary or other tangible form, shall be returned to the Delivering Party only upon the written request of the Delivering Party, if the transaction is not consummated. The Receiving Party may retain the Receiving Party’s Work Product. Retained Information will not be kept confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the Return of Information clause, we introduce a contradiction to the confidentiality requirements outlined in Section 4.B. While Section 4.B dictates that 'The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity', the modified Section 4.D states that 'Retained Information will not be kept confidential.' This creates a direct conflict about whether the confidentiality obligations persist for retained information, leading to legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.D now states that 'Retained Information will not be kept confidential,' which directly contradicts Section 4.B stating 'The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the confidentiality obligations for retained information.

perturbation.original_text: E. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits the Information pursuant to this Confidentiality Agreement, becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, the Receiving Party shall provide the Delivering Party with prompt Notice so that the Delivering Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not sought, or if sought, is not obtained, or that the Delivering Party waives in writing compliance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement, the Receiving Party shall furnish only that portion of the Information which the Receiving Party is compelled to disclose or else stand potentially liable for contempt or suffer any other censure or penalty, as determined by the reasonably supported written legal opinion of its legal counsel, an original of which legal opinion shall be delivered to the Delivering Party, and the Receiving Party shall exercise its best efforts to obtain an order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Information required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: E. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits the Information pursuant to this Confidentiality Agreement, becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, the Receiving Party shall immediately disclose all of the Information, regardless of whether the Delivering Party seeks a protective order or waives compliance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party is not required to seek an order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Information required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party 'shall immediately disclose all of the Information' when legally compelled, removing the requirement to notify the Delivering Party or seek a protective order. This directly contradicts Section 4.B (MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY), which mandates obtaining prior written consent before disclosing information, creating conflict between disclosing all information compulsorily and upholding confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party 'shall immediately disclose all of the Information' when legally compelled, removing the requirement to notify the Delivering Party or seek a protective order. This directly contradicts Section B (MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY), which mandates obtaining prior written consent before disclosing information, creating conflict between disclosing all information compulsorily and upholding confidentiality. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition as the changed section provides conflicting obligations compared to the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: A. CLAIMS: Each of the Seller and the Buyer (“Indemnifying Party”) shall indemnify, defend, with counsel reasonably satisfactory to the other party, and hold the other party free, clear and harmless from and against any and all claims, demands, liens, agreements, contracts, covenants, actions, suits, causes of action, obligations, controversies, debts, fees, penalties, costs, expenses, damages, losses, taxes, fines, injuries, judgments, orders and liabilities, (including, without limitation, damage to property and injuries to or death of any person, reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses, consultants' fees and expenses, expert witness fees and expenses and court fees and costs), of whatsoever character , whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected (collectively, "Claims") arising out of or related to the failure by the Indemnifying Party or its Representatives to strictly and fully comply with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. CLAIMS: Each of the Seller and the Buyer (“Indemnifying Party”) shall indemnify, defend, with counsel reasonably satisfactory to the other party, and hold the other party free, clear and harmless from and against any and all claims, demands, liens, agreements, contracts, covenants, actions, suits, causes of action, obligations, controversies, debts, fees, penalties, costs, expenses, damages, losses, taxes, fines, injuries, judgments, orders and liabilities, (including, without limitation, damage to property and injuries to or death of any person, reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses, consultants' fees and expenses, expert witness fees and expenses and court fees and costs), of whatsoever character , whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected (collectively, "Claims") arising out of or related to the failure by the Indemnifying Party or its Representatives to strictly and fully comply with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement, except for claims arising from a Receiving Party's failure to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the scope of indemnification. Section 5.A originally requires indemnification for failures to comply with the Confidentiality Agreement but this modification excludes 'claims arising from a Receiving Party's failure to maintain confidentiality'. This conflicts with Section 4.B (MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY), which imposes strict obligations on the Receiving Party to maintain confidentiality and makes them responsible for breaches by their Representatives, therefore making it unclear if the Delivering party can even defend themselves if there's a conflict arising due to a disclosure by the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.A adds an exception for claims arising from a Receiving Party's failure to maintain confidentiality. This directly contradicts Section 4.B, which states that 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.' The modification creates a situation where the Receiving Party is responsible for breaches of confidentiality in 4.B, but potentially not indemnified for those same breaches under the modified 5.A. This is a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party concerning confidentiality breaches and indemnification.

perturbation.original_text: K. ASSIGNMENT:This Confidentiality Agreement is not assignable, except with the prior consent of the Delivering Party(ies).
perturbation.changed_text: K. ASSIGNMENT: This Confidentiality Agreement is freely assignable by the Receiving Party without the consent of the Delivering Party(ies).
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts Section 6.M (SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS). Initially, the contract states that it is 'not assignable, except with the prior consent of the Delivering Party(ies).' This is directly negated by allowing the Receiving Party to freely assign the agreement, contradicting the intended restrictions on assignment and creating uncertainty about which terms govern the agreement's transferability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: M. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS:Subject to the limitation set forth in Paragraph 6K above, this Confidentiality Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.K states that the Confidentiality Agreement is 'not assignable, except with the prior consent of the Delivering Party(ies).' The changed text allows the Receiving Party to freely assign the agreement without the consent of the Delivering Party(ies). This directly contradicts the original restriction on assignment. Section 6.M (SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS) states that the agreement is binding upon successors and assigns 'Subject to the limitation set forth in Paragraph 6K above'. Because the changed text removes the limitation, it creates a direct contradiction. Therefore, the change causes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: L. CONSENTS AND APPROVALS: Unless expressly provided otherwise, wherever in this Confidentiality Agreement the consent or approval of a party is required, such consent or approval must be in writing, and it may be given or withheld in the sole and absolute discretion of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: L. CONSENTS AND APPROVALS: Unless expressly provided otherwise, wherever in this Confidentiality Agreement the consent or approval of a party is required, such consent or approval may be given orally, and it may be unreasonably withheld or delayed by such party. The party providing the approval has 10 days to submit it in writing, but this submission is optional.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the requirement for written consent outlined in Section 6.B (NOTICES). The original Section 6.L states that all consents and approvals must be in writing; however, this modification states that consents can be given orally with the option for written submission. Since the notices must be written as described in 6.B, it created an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. NOTICES:Any notice or communication required or permitted by this Confidentiality Agreement (“Notice”) shall be deemed sufficiently given if it is given in writing addressed as provided in the Notice information on the signature pages, and shall be effective upon the earlier of when it is actually received by the party to whom it is directed, or when the period set forth below expires (whether or not it is actually received):(1) If delivered personally, when it is delivered; (2)If transmitted by facsimile transmission (“Fax”), then, provided the party immediately sends a confirmation copy by first class mail, upon: (i) transmission to the party’ s Fax number set forth below, with the party’s name and address set forth below clearly shown on
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.L states that all consents and approvals must be in writing. The changed text allows for oral consents and approvals, directly contradicting the requirement for written notices outlined in Section 6.B. Section 6.B requires all notices to be in writing. The changed text therefore creates a clear in-text contradiction.
